# Copilot instructions for SerialCommunication

## Quick commands

- Build (Visual Studio): Open `SerialCommunication.slnx` in Visual Studio 2019/2022 and use Build > Build Solution.
- Build (CLI / Developer Command Prompt):
  - msbuild "SerialCommunication.slnx" /p:Configuration=Debug
  - or for a Release build: msbuild "SerialCommunication.slnx" /p:Configuration=Release
- Arduino build & upload (arduino-cli):
  - Compile: `arduino-cli compile --fqbn arduino:avr:uno arduino`
  - Upload (replace COM3): `arduino-cli upload -p COM3 --fqbn arduino:avr:uno arduino`

- Tests: No automated tests present in this repo.
- Linting: No repository-specific linter configured.

## High-level architecture

- Desktop app: A C# WinForms application (project: `SerialCommunication/SerialCommunication.csproj`). Entry point is `Program.cs` which launches `Form1`.
  - Form1 enumerates serial ports and exposes controls for connecting and sending commands. UI assets live under `SerialCommunication/Resources`.
  - Settings are stored in `SerialCommunication/Properties/Settings.settings`.
- Embedded firmware: Arduino code lives in `arduino/`.
  - Sketch: `SerialCommunication.ino` implements a text-based command protocol using the bundled `SerialCommand` helper (`SerialCommand.h/.cpp`).
  - Helper: `analog.c` provides analog-read helper `analogReadDelay()`.
- Communication: ASCII text commands terminated by a newline. Commands include `set`, `toggle`, `get`, `ping`, `help`, `debug`.
  - Responses: examples: `pong`, `set done`, `d{pin}: {0|1}`, `a{pin}: {value}`.
  - Default baudrate: 115200 (Arduino sketch and Form1 set this value).

## Key conventions and repository notes

- Serial protocol expectations:
  - Commands are tokenized by spaces, terminated with a newline character. The Arduino `SerialCommand` implementation expects `\n` as the terminator.
  - Digital outputs: pins 2..4. PWM outputs: 9..11. Digital inputs: 5..7. Analog inputs: A0..A5.
- Project file: solution filename uses `.slnx` — open with Visual Studio. Prefer building in the Visual Studio Developer Command Prompt (msbuild) if not using the IDE.
- Prebuilt artifacts: `bin/`, `obj/` and `.vs/` are present in the repo. These are generated by Visual Studio and not needed for source edits.

## Files and places to check when extending

- UI: `SerialCommunication/Form1.cs` and designer `Form1.Designer.cs`.
- Serial protocol handlers and Arduino command mapping: `arduino/SerialCommunication.ino`, `arduino/SerialCommand.*`.
- Settings and resources: `SerialCommunication/Properties/` and `SerialCommunication/Resources/`.
